course_description:
This is the final clinical placement course in the Master of Speech Pathology Studies program. Students attend a block placement in a workplace clinic to consolidate their case-based knowledge and integration, their clinical management skills and their professional role within clinical settings.

This is the final clinical placement course in the Master of Speech Pathology Studies program. Students are rostered to a paediatric or adult external clinic. During the semester, all studentsﾠwill engage in self-directed learning with a specific emphasis on reflection and application of knowledge, skills and experience gained in their clinical placement and the development of professional and personal skills toward the transition to the workforce, culminating in an oral viva examination at the end of the semester exam period.

All students must achieve entry-level competency (as evaluated by Competency Assessment in Speech Pathology - COMPASSﾮ2) and pass the end-of-semester oral viva examination to pass this course.

timetable:
Additional timetable information
Once your clinical placement details are known, please ensure that you contact your practice educator to confirm your attendance at the clinic.
course_aims: The aim of this clinical course is for students to demonstrate entry level competency in speech pathology practice with clients across the lifespan presenting with communication and swallowing disorders and to reflect on their own clinical practice and service delivery requirements to meet the needs of individuals and communities.

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate effective communication skills that demonstrate a high level of professionalism and are appropriate to the audience (SPA PS Domains/Standards 1.1, 1.5; CBOS Unit 2).
Critically reflect on your professional practice, clinical reasoning and communication skills to inform your ongoing professional development (SPA PS Domains/Standards 2.1, 2.2, 2.3; CBOS Unit 7).

Demonstrate entry-level skills in speech pathology practice, including the assessment, diagnosis, planning, implementation and evaluation of speech pathology services (SPA PS Domains/Standards 3.1 3.5; CBOS Units 1 - 5).
Engage in interprofessional collaborative practice to achieve effective client and community outcomes (SPA PS Domains/Standards 1.3, 1.4, 1.6, 1.7, 3.1, 3.4; CBOS Units 2, 4, 5, 6, 7).
Provide evidence-based rationales for the management approach for clients with communication and/or swallowing difficulties (SPA PS Domains/Standards 1.1, 1.3, 2.2, 2.5, 3.4; CBOS Unit 5).

assessment_details.title: End of Placement assessment
assessment_details.due_date: COMPASS®2 Online will be used for this clinical course. Practice educators should complete their final assessments for students 1 week after the completion of the placement.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Student clinical competencies will be assessed using COMPASS®2 Online at the mid- and end point of the placement. Students must pass the clinical placement evaluation according to COMPASS®2 criteria in order to pass the course. See SPCH7816 Clinical Course Booklet on the SPCH7816 Blackboard site for criteria for a passing grade for this assessment. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Students will be assessed using COMPASSﾮ2 Online.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessment_details.title: Oral Exam - Clinical Viva
assessment_details.due_date: 9/06/2025 Details regarding the start time for your viva will be available in week 13.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This oral examination will be held at a date in the second week of the end of semester exam period. This will be an individual oral examination with one speech pathology staff member. Viva duration will be 35 minutes, including 15 minutes of planning time. Students will receive written information about two unseen client cases, one paediatric and one adult. Students will have the opportunity to familiarise themselves with the written case details just prior to entering the viva. Within the viva, students will respond to questions relating to the cases and discuss their proposed assessment and management. Further details about the case preparation, potential discussion points and the assessment of student performance in the clinical viva will be provided during semester. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.exam_details: Planning time 15 minutes Duration 20 minutes Calculator options No calculators permitted Open/closed book Open Book examination Exam platform Other Invigilation Invigilated in person
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Deferral or extension
You may be able to defer this exam .
A request for a deferral of an exam must be submitted by lodging the appropriate (online) form with supporting documentation via mySI-net > My Requests no later than five (5) calendar days after the date of the original exam. Students are encouraged to familiarise themselves with the information on deferring an exam available on my.UQ (https://my.uq.edu.au/information-and-services/manage-my-program/exams-and-assessment/deferring-exam). Deferral of the SPCH7816 clinical viva will impact on the student’s progression through the program and therefore final graduation will be delayed.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam .

learning_resources.library_resources: Library resources are available on the UQ Library website .

learning_activities.topic: Clinical viva preparation workshops Students will participate in a workshop session designed to prepare them for their clinical viva in the end of semester exam period. This workshop will provide students with an opportunity to gain further details about the viva, and discuss the content and structure. Details regarding the timing of this workshop will be provided during semester. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

Block clinical practicum in external setting This is the final clinical placement course in the Master of Speech Pathology Studies program. Students are rostered to a paediatric or adult external clinic. Start and finish dates may vary as negotiated with students and practice facilities in a speech pathology environment. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
